title: Robin Hood Gets 'Logan' Treatment in First Reactions to Action-Adventure Thriller

blocks.content: Logan star Hugh Jackman brings the same aged hero approach to another beloved character, Robin Hood, in the new action-adventure thriller, The Death of Robin Hood, and the first reactions reveal that it will live up to the legacy of both the (at one time) final Wolverine outing and the folklore hero on which it’s based. Legacy sequels have become all the rage in recent years, not least due to the sheer level of success found by Jackman’s Marvel outing, and it sounds like The Death of Robin Hood has even more rage than most.

Directed by A Quiet Place: Day One and Pig’s Michael Sarnoski, The Death of Robin Hood is a long-held passion project for the filmmaker and features an all-star ensemble cast that includes Jodie Comer (Killing Eve, 28 Years Later), Bill Skarsgård (Nosferatu, IT: Welcome to Derry), Murray Bartlett (The Last of Us), and Noah Jupe (A Quiet Place) alongside Jackman. Based on the 17th-century ballad "Robin Hood's Death," the bleak thriller picks up with the legendary outlaw as he grapples with his past after a life of crime and murder. Finding himself gravely injured, he’s taken in by a mysterious woman and is offered a chance at salvation.

Critic Courtney Howard declares that The Death of Robin Hood “RIPS!” calling it Bold & brutal, this is a kinetic, brilliant new generational legend and a profound rumination on mortality. Hugh Jackman, Jodie Comer & Bill Skarsgård are unassailably exceptional. Pat Scola’s cinematography, exhilarative & painterly.” Further praise is heaped on Jackman by Matt Neglia of @NextBestPicture, who highlights his “raw intensity, ruggedness, and weathered gravitas.”

"Bleak even by Michael Sarnoski’s standards, the first third is brutal, muddy, and action-heavy while the remaining two thirds are a somber character study that deconstructs the Robin Hood myth to show us how one man chooses to face his mortality."

Darren Movie Reviews, meanwhile, commended the movie for “Balancing a brutal rage and reflectiveness in a character driven drama,” that “shares more similarities to James Mangold’s Logan than you would expect.” However, not everyone was quite as enamored, with @AdriCaporusso saying it “starts off so grizzly and epic that I thought we had something truly incredible here, but the promise starts to wash away as the melancholy of its subject doesn't reveal a clear emotional thesis for its complicated figure.” Similarly, @lucam8a slammed The Death of Robin Hood as “the snore-thman,” adding it’s “really great for about 30 minutes or so, only to become a complete slog.”

But, unlike the movie itself (the title is a bit of a spoiler), we shall end on a high note, with @theholofiles also likening The Death of Robin Hood to Logan and calling it brutal, brooding, and bleak.

"THE DEATH OF ROBIN HOOD does for Robin Hood what LOGAN did for Wolverine. It’s a brutal, brooding, bleak, and slow (at times too slow) character study and reimagining of the legendary outlaw that reconceptualizes him as a morally fractured figure, rather than a hero. Hugh Jackman brings such anguish, cruelty, and unexpected warmth to cinema’s most complex depiction of Robin Hood to date."
The Death of Robin Hood is due to premiere in theaters on June 19, 2026, courtesy of A24.
